content_en:
Al-Isabah fi Tamyiz as-Sahabah — Hitting the Mark in Distinguishing the Companions — is the most comprehensive biographical dictionary of the Companions of the Prophet Muhammad ever compiled. Its title captures its purpose: to achieve precision in determining who was and who was not a Companion, resolving the ambiguities and contradictions in the earlier biographical literature and establishing firm criteria for the determination of Companionship.

Ibn Hajar's definition of a Companion follows the methodology of the hadith scholars: a Companion is any Muslim who met the Prophet while believing in him and died as a Muslim. This definition is broader than some earlier formulations and more restrictive than others, and Ibn Hajar argues for it carefully at the beginning of the work, drawing on the opinions of the great hadith authorities. The precision of the definition is necessary because the status of Companion carries theological and legal weight: the testimony of a Companion is accepted without scrutiny, their narrations of hadith carry special authority, and their status as members of the best generation is part of Islamic doctrine.

The work is organized alphabetically by first name, with subsidiary sections for those known only by kunya (teknonym), laqab (epithet), or as being a member of a group. A separate section addresses women Companions. For each entry, Ibn Hajar cites the evidence for the person's Companionship, provides biographical information where available, notes the hadith they are reported to have transmitted, and resolves discrepancies between earlier accounts.

Ibn Hajar also incorporates a critical evaluation of the earlier biographical works on the Companions, including those of Ibn Abd al-Barr (al-Isti'ab), Ibn al-Athir (Usd al-Ghaba), and others. He notes where they have erred, where they have included people who were not Companions, and where they have omitted known Companions. This critical apparatus makes al-Isabah not only a reference but a scholarly assessment of the entire genre.
content_id:
Al-Isabah fi Tamyiz ash-Shahabah — Mencapai Sasaran dalam Membedakan Para Sahabat — adalah kamus biografi Para Sahabat Nabi Muhammad ﷺ yang paling komprehensif yang pernah disusun. Judulnya mencerminkan tujuannya: mencapai ketepatan dalam menentukan siapa yang merupakan Sahabat dan siapa yang bukan, menyelesaikan ambiguitas dan kontradiksi dalam literatur biografi terdahulu serta menetapkan kriteria yang kuat untuk penentuan status Sahabat.

Definisi Ibn Hajar tentang seorang Sahabat mengikuti metodologi ulama hadis: seorang Sahabat adalah setiap Muslim yang bertemu Nabi ﷺ dalam keadaan beriman kepada beliau dan wafat sebagai seorang Muslim. Definisi ini lebih luas dari beberapa formulasi terdahulu dan lebih ketat dari yang lainnya, dan Ibn Hajar berargumen untuk definisi ini dengan cermat di awal karya, berdasarkan pendapat-pendapat otoritas hadis besar. Ketepatan definisi ini diperlukan karena status Sahabat membawa bobot teologis dan hukum: kesaksian seorang Sahabat diterima tanpa pemeriksaan mendalam, riwayat-riwayat hadis mereka membawa otoritas khusus, dan status mereka sebagai anggota generasi terbaik adalah bagian dari akidah Islam.

Karya ini disusun secara alfabetis berdasarkan nama pertama, dengan bagian-bagian tambahan untuk mereka yang hanya dikenal dari kunya (teknonym), laqab (julukan), atau sebagai anggota suatu kelompok. Bagian terpisah membahas Sahabat perempuan. Untuk setiap entri, Ibn Hajar mengutip bukti status Kesahabatan seseorang, menyediakan informasi biografis yang tersedia, mencatat hadis yang mereka riwayatkan, dan menyelesaikan ketidaksesuaian antara keterangan-keterangan terdahulu.

Ibn Hajar juga memasukkan evaluasi kritis atas karya-karya biografi terdahulu tentang Para Sahabat, termasuk karya Ibn Abd al-Barr (al-Isti'ab), Ibn al-Atsir (Usd al-Ghabah), dan lainnya. Beliau mencatat di mana mereka keliru, di mana mereka memasukkan orang-orang yang bukan Sahabat, dan di mana mereka menghilangkan Sahabat yang dikenal. Perangkat kritis ini menjadikan Al-Isabah bukan sekadar kumpulan biografi melainkan penilaian kritis terhadap seluruh tradisi beasiswa tentang Para Sahabat.

Skala karya ini luar biasa: lebih dari dua belas ribu entri, diorganisir dengan hati-hati dan masing-masing tunduk pada analisis evidensial yang sama yang menjadi ciri khas metode ilmiah Ibn Hajar di tempat lain. Bahwa karya ini diselesaikan sama sekali adalah pencapaian yang luar biasa; bahwa diselesaikan dengan standar keandalan yang tinggi menempatkannya di antara puncak-puncak beasiswa Islam.
content_ar:
Al-Isabah fi Tamyiz as-Sahabah — Hitting the Mark in Distinguishing the Companions — is the most comprehensive biographical dictionary of the Companions of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم Muhammad ever compiled. Its title captures its purpose: to achieve precision in determining who was and who was not a Companion, resolving the ambiguities and contradictions in the earlier biographical literature and establishing firm criteria for the determination of Companionship.

Ibn Hajar's definition of a Companion follows the methodology of the hadith scholars: a Companion is any Muslim who met النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم while believing in him and died as a Muslim. This definition is broader than some earlier formulations and more restrictive than others, and Ibn Hajar argues for it carefully at the beginning of the work, drawing on the opinions of the great hadith authorities. The precision of the definition is necessary because the status of Companion carries theological and legal weight: the testimony of a Companion is accepted without scrutiny, their narrations of hadith carry special authority, and their status as members of the best generation is part of Islamic doctrine.

The work is organized alphabetically by first name, with subsidiary sections for those known only by kunya (teknonym), laqab (epithet), or as being a member of a group. A separate section addresses women Companions. For each entry, Ibn Hajar cites the evidence for the person's Companionship, provides biographical information where available, notes the hadith they are reported to have transmitted, and resolves discrepancies between earlier accounts.

Ibn Hajar also incorporates a critical evaluation of the earlier biographical works on the Companions, including those of Ibn Abd al-Barr (al-Isti'ab), Ibn al-Athir (Usd al-Ghaba), and others. He notes where they have erred, where they have included people who were not Companions, and where they have omitted known Companions. This critical apparatus makes al-Isabah not only a reference but a scholarly assessment of the entire genre.
